key_insight: DeepSpot-M is a multimodal foundation model that turns routine histology into a queryable, transcriptome-wide virtual spatial transcriptomics map, including genes not seen during training.
clinical_relevance: This could make molecular profiling far more scalable across routine pathology slides, helping reveal tumor biology and biomarker signals in patients who do not receive physical spatial transcriptomics.
method: Represents genes with embeddings from foundation models spanning DNA, RNA, proteins, single cells, and biomedical text.
Reformulates prediction as a gene-query task to span the full protein-coding transcriptome.
Uses test-time adaptation to transfer from pan-cancer training data to new cohorts and assays.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong transfer to held-out cancers and a virtual atlas of 28,664 TCGA slides across 32 cancers, with recovery of a pan-cancer malignancy map.
watch_item: Because outputs are computational inferences from histology, real-world utility will depend on robust external validation and careful control of cohort, stain, and site variability.
paper_title: DeepSpot-M: a multimodal foundation model for transcriptome-wide virtual spatial transcriptomics from histology
